method: OpenAlex citation graph — GET /works/doi:10.1038/s41597-025-04511-0 resolved to W4408197500, then GET /works?filter=cites:W4408197500&per_page=25&select=id,doi,title,publication_year,cited_by_count. Both calls succeeded (no rate-limit this run). Task/method labels below were assigned by reading each citing work's OpenAlex-provided title, primary_topic, and reconstructed abstract (from abstract_inverted_index).

citing_works.title: On a confusion about there being two types of consciousness
citing_works.task: theoretical/conceptual citation (phenomenal vs. access consciousness debate)

citing_works.title: Common Phenomenal and Neural Substrate Geometry in Visual Motion Perception
citing_works.task: neural-phenomenal correspondence / representational geometry, extended to visual motion (not color)
citing_works.method: structural/relational qualia characterization compared to neural representational geometry

citing_works.title: Robust individual alignment of color qualia structures: toward a structure-based taxonomy of divergent color experiences
citing_works.task: subtyping (individual-to-individual color qualia structure alignment / taxonomy of typical vs. divergent color experiences)
citing_works.method: unsupervised structural alignment (Gromov-Wasserstein-type) of pairwise color-similarity structures across individuals
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: likely - same paradigm/lab, builds a taxonomy from qualia-structure alignment

citing_works.title: Systematic comparison of color representations between humans and deep neural networks: towards predicting human color perception in a vast color space
citing_works.task: foundation-model / representational-alignment (benchmarking DNN color representations against human color similarity structure)
citing_works.method: human-vs-DNN representational comparison across a large color space
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: likely - uses human color-similarity judgments as ground truth for DNN comparison

citing_works.title: Qualia structures collapse for geometric shapes, but not faces, when spatial attention is withdrawn
citing_works.task: biomarker/subtyping (attentional modulation of qualia-structure alignment), paradigm extended to geometric shapes and faces
citing_works.method: similarity-rating paradigm under attentional manipulation + structural alignment analysis (same lab, different stimulus classes)

citing_works.title: A multi-level visual representation dataset for large-scale non-financial information disclosure
citing_works.task: unrelated domain (corporate ESG/sustainability-report visual analysis); appears to cite only as a Scientific Data data-descriptor precedent

citing_works.title: Measuring the Diversity of Qualia: Category-Theoretic Indices for Psychophysical Experimental Data
citing_works.task: subtyping/methods-development (quantifying diversity of qualia structures between groups, e.g. color-typical vs. atypical)
citing_works.method: category-theoretic diversity indices applied to pairwise-similarity dissimilarity matrices
citing_works.uses_dataset_directly: likely - same paradigm's similarity-rating data used as worked example

dominant_tasks: color-qualia structural analysis / subtyping (individual-differences alignment, taxonomy of divergent color experiences, diversity quantification) — mostly same-lab (Tsuchiya/Oizumi group) follow-ups
human-vs-deep-neural-network representational comparison (foundation-model benchmarking against human color similarity structure)
paradigm extension to new stimulus domains (visual motion, geometric shapes, faces) without directly reusing this dataset's color-qualia data
theoretical/conceptual citation (consciousness-structure reviews and opinion pieces, no direct data reuse)
saturation_rationale: Only 7 works cite this data descriptor via OpenAlex's citation graph, roughly 16 months post-publication. Just 2-3 works plausibly reanalyze the underlying similarity-judgment data directly (the individual-alignment taxonomy preprint, the human-vs-DNN color comparison preprint, and the category-theoretic diversity-index paper), and these are dominated by the originating lab (Tsuchiya, Oizumi, and close collaborators) rather than independent external groups. Two works extend the qualia-structure paradigm methodologically to new, unrelated stimulus domains (visual motion; geometric shapes/faces) without touching this dataset's actual color-similarity or fMRI data. One citation is purely theoretical/conceptual (consciousness-type debate), and one appears unrelated in domain (ESG/sustainability visual-disclosure dataset), likely citing this work only as a Scientific Data data-descriptor exemplar. No external-validation, segmentation, outcome-prediction, or causal-inference use has appeared, and no independent group outside the original team has reanalyzed the with/without-report neuroimaging (fMRI) contrast itself. This indicates the dataset is still in an early, largely single-group exploitation phase with substantial white space for independent secondary analyses (e.g., decoding qualia structure directly from the fMRI data, report-vs-no-report neural correlate analyses, or cross-lab replication).
